isolation.serialization: Focused cargo tests executed with --test-threads 1 against the shared embedded DB fixture state

assertions.title: Unified raw-row decode helper handles string and object record links

steps.action: Read validation-contract.md and cargo-test flow guidance
steps.expected: Clear focused cargo-test evidence requirements for VAL-HYDRATE-007
steps.observed: Contract requires terminal-output, shared save/read hydration consistency, mixed string/object record-link fixtures, and proof of a single decode helper/seam.

steps.expected: A focused regression proves mixed object-form branch links and string-form root links decode consistently through get/get_record/list/list_limit
steps.observed: Passed: 1 test, 0 failed in 0.96s. The test seeds an object-form branch link and a string-form root link via raw SQL, then asserts get/get_record/list/list_limit all return the same fully hydrated ItNestedForeignRoot graph.

steps.expected: Alias-backed foreign reads reuse the same decode semantics across save/get/get_record/list/list_limit
steps.observed: Passed: 1 test, 0 failed in 0.98s. The test verifies save return, get, get_record, list, and list_limit all match the same aliased nested holder graph while raw rows store RecordId references in target tables.

steps.action: Inspect the decode seam in core/src/lib.rs and focused tests in core/tests/integration_db.rs
steps.expected: One canonical stored-row decode boundary is reused across caller-facing entry points
steps.observed: Confirmed a dedicated seam: core/src/lib.rs exposes decode_stored_record_links() backed by rewrite_foreign_record_link_value(), and integration_db.rs explicitly states get_record/list/list_limit should reuse the shared decode helper.

terminalOutput: test result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 73 filtered out; finished in 0.96s
test result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 73 filtered out; finished in 0.98s
evidence.rawDbCheck: decode_raw_row_helper_accepts_string_and_object_record_links seeds object-form leaf links and string-form branch links with raw SQL before hydrate assertions; table_as_alias_with_nested_foreign_roundtrips_across_all_reads asserts raw alias and holder rows store RecordId references in target tables.
evidence.apiResult: Shared hydration consistency verified across save return, get(), get_record(), list(), and list_limit() for the focused decode-boundary and alias-backed nested foreign cases.
testProof: core/src/lib.rs: decode_stored_record_links() delegates to rewrite_foreign_record_link_value() as the stored-row decode seam for record-link normalization.

core/tests/integration_db.rs: decode_raw_row_helper_accepts_string_and_object_record_links exercises mixed string/object record-link fixtures and asserts shared helper reuse across get/get_record/list/list_limit.
core/tests/integration_db.rs: table_as_alias_with_nested_foreign_roundtrips_across_all_reads proves alias-backed nested foreign reads share the same caller-facing decode semantics.

summary: VAL-HYDRATE-007 passed. Focused cargo-test evidence shows the unified stored-row decode boundary handles mixed string/object record links and preserves consistent hydration across save/get/get_record/list/list_limit, including alias-backed nested foreign reads.
